compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED + FIXED (landed change 351 / Change-Id I246b286, merged to main).

Raw completion-verifier output captured at runtime (instrumented validate_to, model claude-sonnet-4-6, 3 bounded-retry attempts on ticket 81b4) — each was a valid top-level JSON ARRAY whose sole dict element is the real verdict, preceded by echoed intermediate arrays the reasoning model emitted:
  attempt 1: [["open","in_progress"], {"verdict":"PASS","findings":[],"summary":"..."}]
  attempt 2: [["open","in_progress"], {"verdict":"PASS","findings":[]}]
  attempt 3: [["idea","open","in_progress","blocked","closed","archived","deleted"], ["open","in_progress"], {"verdict":"PASS","findings":[]}]

Mechanism: tolerant_parse's json.loads accepts the whole array as a list; validate_to only unwrapped a length-1 [{}], so [noise, {verdict}] was rejected 'got list' on every retry -> fail-closed close gate. Fix: validate_to now recovers the list's SOLE dict element (zero-dict and multi-dict still rejected). RED->GREEN + mutation-checked tests in tests/unit/test_structured.py. Verified end-to-end: all 5 idea-status stories + epic jira-reb-686 subsequently closed through the real verifier (the original 'got list' no longer reproduces).

compiled_state.description:
The completion-verification close gate fails to produce a verdict — and therefore
**blocks closing any work ticket** (fail-closed) — when the completion-verifier LLM
returns its structured output as a top-level JSON **list** instead of the expected
`completion_verdict` object. The LLM is available and the call runs; the failure is in
parsing/validating its response shape, not in the ticket's actual completion state.

Discovered while trying to close the five `idea`-status stories after they merged to
`main` (epic jira-reb-686). Reproduced deterministically across 3 attempts on
`81b4-985e-b3e8-4026`; the same gate runs for every work-ticket close, so it is not
specific to that ticket.

## Exact error

```
Error: cannot close 81b4-985e-b3e8-4026: completion verification could not run
(completion verification workflow did not produce a verdict: step 'decide@then/verify'
failed: structured output must be a JSON object, got list). The completion-verification
gate is enabled (verify.require_completion_verification_for_close); install the 'agents'
extra and set a model API key, or override with --force-close="<reason>".
```

## Where it happens (code map)

- **Raised at** `src/rebar/llm/structured.py:179`, in `validate_to(model_cls, data)`:
  when the parsed structured output is not a `dict` it raises
  `StructuredOutputError("structured output must be a JSON object, got {type}")` —
  here `type == list`.
- **Existing partial handling** `src/rebar/llm/structured.py:168-175`: a *single*-element
  list wrapping one object (`[ {...} ]`) is already unwrapped to `{...}` (prior fixes
  "artsy-chain-hold / dash-lure-slag"). A **multi-element list, a list of non-dicts, or
  an empty list is still rejected** — that is the case hit here.
- **Failing step** `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/completion-verification.yaml`, step
  `decide` → `then` → `verify`: `prompt: completion-verifier`, `mode: structured`,
  `output_schema: completion_verdict`.
- **Expected contract** `completion_verdict` = `{verdict: "PASS"|"FAIL", findings: [...]}`
  (see `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` `_OUTPUT_SCHEMA = "completion_verdict"`,
  `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py:156`, and the citation shape in
  `src/rebar/llm/findings.py`).
- **Model/runner** verifier default model `claude-sonnet-4-6`
  (`src/rebar/llm/config.py:41` `VERIFIER_DEFAULT_MODEL`); structured runner is
  pydantic_ai (same as plan-review).

## Root-cause hypothesis

The verifier model emitted its answer as a **top-level JSON array** rather than the
wrapping verdict object — most likely a bare findings array
(`[{finding}, {finding}, …]`) or a per-criterion list, instead of
`{verdict, findings: [...]}`. `81b4` carries **13 `## Acceptance Criteria` checkboxes**,
which may nudge the model toward returning a per-criterion list. The single-element
unwrap at `structured.py:174` does not cover a multi-element / non-`[{}]` list, so
`validate_to` rejects it, the `verify` step produces no verdict, and the fail-closed gate
blocks the close.

Open question: whether the **layer-4 bounded retry** (the StructuredOutputError message
is meant to be fed back to the model for one retry) is actually wired on the workflow
`mode: structured` runner path (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_ops.py` /
`src/rebar/llm/runner.py`). The failure was **deterministic across 3 close attempts**,
which suggests either the retry is not firing on this path, or the model reliably repeats
the list for this context.

## Missing diagnostic (do this first next session)

The raw LLM output is **not surfaced or persisted** on this failure path — the CLI close
does not emit a `run_id`/trace, so we cannot see the exact list the model returned. The
first troubleshooting step is to **capture the raw model output** (e.g. add temporary
logging in `validate_to`/the structured runner, or run
`rebar verify-completion 81b4-985e-b3e8-4026` directly if that surfaces the payload) to
confirm whether it is a bare findings array, a per-criterion list, an empty list, or a
multi-element wrapper.

## Reproduction Steps

1. On a checkout with the `[agents]` extra + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` set, and
   `rebar.toml` `verify.require_completion_verification_for_close = true` (this repo's
   default).
2. Take any `in_progress` work ticket whose criteria are genuinely met (e.g.
   `81b4-985e-b3e8-4026`, now merged to `main`).
3. Run `rebar transition <id> in_progress closed`.
4. Observe the close is refused with the "structured output must be a JSON object, got
   list" error above. Deterministic across retries.

- Expected: the completion verifier returns a `completion_verdict` object; a PASS closes
  (and signs when certifiable), a FAIL blocks with per-criterion findings.
- Actual: the verifier's structured output is a JSON list; `validate_to` rejects it; the
  gate produces no verdict and fail-closes, blocking the close entirely.

## Impact

Because the gate is fail-closed, this blocks closing **every** work ticket whenever the
verifier returns a list — the only escapes are `--force-close="<reason>"` (closes
**unsigned/uncertified**) or disabling the gate. It blocked closing all five idea-status
stories and the epic (jira-reb-686) after they merged.

## Candidate fixes to evaluate (not yet chosen)

1. **Capture + log** the raw model output on `StructuredOutputError` (diagnosability first).
2. **Verify/repair the retry**: ensure the bounded retry with the "must be a JSON object"
   feedback actually fires on the workflow `mode: structured` path; strengthen the retry
   prompt to demand the `{verdict, findings}` wrapper.
3. **Prompt hardening**: make the `completion-verifier` prompt explicitly require a single
   top-level object and never a bare array.
4. **Bounded structural repair (careful)**: if the output is a list of finding-shaped
   dicts, wrap as `{verdict: FAIL, findings: [...]}` (non-empty) — but this changes
   semantics and must not mask a genuine PASS; evaluate against the completion-verifier
   eval spec (`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/completion-verifier.eval.yaml`) before adopting.

## Environment

- nava-rebar 0.7.1; local build at commit 4ca9d8211 (idea-status stack tip; the same gate
  code is on `main`).
- Gate config: `rebar.toml` `verify.require_completion_verification_for_close = true`.
- Verifier model: `claude-sonnet-4-6` (VERIFIER_DEFAULT_MODEL); pydantic_ai runner.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The raw completion-verifier LLM output for a reproducing case is captured and
      recorded (what list shape it returns).
- [ ] The completion-verification gate produces a valid `completion_verdict` when the
      model returns a list-shaped answer — either via a robust retry that recovers the
      `{verdict, findings}` object, prompt hardening that prevents the list, or a
      safe bounded repair — without masking a genuine PASS or FAIL.
- [ ] `structured.validate_to` (or the structured runner) handles the non-single-element
      list case deterministically (documented behavior + unit test), rather than only the
      `[{one dict}]` case.
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the "got list" verifier output and asserts the gate now
      yields a verdict (or a deterministic, documented outcome).
- [ ] Closing a genuinely-complete work ticket with the gate enabled succeeds (verified on
      one of the idea-status stories or an equivalent).
- [ ] `make lint` and the affected tests pass.
compiled_state.title: Completion-verifier gate fails closed on list-shaped LLM structured output ('got list')
